properties.description:
Previously known as Interxion STO1. Interxion was acquired by Digital Realty in 2022 and rebranded its data centers to the Digital Realty brand in 2022.

Interxion STO1 is conveniently located 15km north of Stockholm in Kista, the centre of Sweden's ICT industry.

Interxion offers:
- A growing community of carriers, ISPs, Internet Exchanges and other connectivity partners helps you reduce the cost of network access and improve performance
- Access to the major carriers and ISPs and direct access to the Swedish Internet Exchange, Netnod
- Dual-entry fibre and SLA-based cross connects

Interxion provides managed power with in-built resilience, allowing extra equipment to be added without outage. Features include:
- N+1 UPS redundancy
- A full range of AC and DC output currents
- High density power configuration
- N+1 backup generators supporting 36 hours at full capacity with high-speed refuelling
- Up to 99.999% availability SLAs on power

Interxion data centres typically have five layers of physical security with trained security staff on site 24/7 and ISO and BS certified for information security management systems.
The facility has world-class protection against fire through fire-retardant walls, early warning laser smoke detectors (VESDA), direct lines to fire stations, and automatic gas-based fire suppression systems.
All equipment is maintained and monitored in a climate-controlled environment 24/7 and there are SLAs on temperature and humidity in line with ASHRAE recommendations.

Interxion is committed to the use of sustainable energy such as free cooling, remote sea water cooling and waste heat re-use. Interxion's Stockholm facilities use 100% renewable energy. When sea water cooling was implemented in our Stockholm data centre in January 2009, it was one of the first seawater cooling systems in the world used to cool a data centre.
This data centre now operates at an exceptionally low and constant PUE, and is not subject to PUE fluctuations caused by an increase in energy to power the chillers during a hot summer.


Finance Community
Interxion is a specialist provider of colocation and proximity hosting to the financial services market. Interxion's financial community hosts a growing number of capital market participants, including investment firms, high-frequency trading firms, hedge funds, brokers and banks.
They benefit from the presence of financial services providers, including leading vendors of market data, DMA and sponsored access, low latency connectivity, leading-edge technology and clearing services.
Interxion provides connectivity to the Burgundy Exchange, Nordics Derivatives Exchange, Nasdaq OMX, and other financial centres such as London, Frankfurt and Zurich.
Data centre customers benefit from over 45 network providers of low-latency connectivity, as well as a Test Lab.
Previously known as Interxion STO5. Interxion was acquired by Digital Realty in 2022 and rebranded its data centers to the Digital Realty brand in 2022.
Previously known as Interxion STO4. Interxion was acquired by Digital Realty in 2022 and rebranded its data centers to the Digital Realty brand in 2022.

Previously known as Interxion STO6. Interxion was acquired by Digital Realty in 2022 and rebranded its data centers to the Digital Realty brand in 2022.

City Hosting provides cheap and good hosting.

- We can provide from single 1U rack space to multiple racks.
- We can provide transit (no BGP announcement fees) or you can bring your own transit, or both.
- No cross-connect costs (8x Cat6 copper and 6 pais singlemode fiber in each rack by default)
- DWDM wavelenght(s) to STOKAB KN7 can be provided for access to STOKABs fiber network and LOTS of carriers/ISPs.

City Hosting provides cheap and good hosting.
We can provide from single 1U rack space to multiple racks.
No cross-connect costs (8x Cat6 copper and 6 pais singlemode fiber in each rack by default)
We can provide transit (no BGP announcement fees) or you can bring your own transit, or both.
DWDM wavelenght(s) to STOKAB KN7 can be provided for access to STOKABs fiber network and LOTS of carriers/ISPs.

Stockholm 3 North consists of 5 computer rooms, all of which are separate fire cells EI60. The facility consists of 5 computer rooms, has a capacity of 6 MW and a total area of 3 400 sqm. The data center is climate repsonsible with security class 3/resistance class 4 shell protection, runs on renewable energy, heat recovery to district heating and certified Fossil Free Data, ISO 9001, 14001, 27001 as well as PCI/DSS (service provider) and audited ISAE 3402 (SOC 1 type 2).

Stockholm South is a 4 MW and 2000 sqm climate neutral data center with security class 3/resistance class 4 shell protection, runs on renewable energy, heat recovery to district heating and certified Fossil Free Data, ISO 9001, 14001, 27001 as well as PCI/DSS (service provider) and audited ISAE 3402 (SOC 1 type 2).

Stockholm 4 South, a cornerstone of Conapto's Stockholm South Campus, offer top-tier colocation services and a flexible infrastructure. This expansive facility seamlessly integrates with Stockholm 2 South, offering a staggering 7600 square meters of computer room space and total 24 MW of power.  Its flexible design includes a 500 square meter retail colocation computer room while the remaining white space awaits bespoke customer solutions tailored to unique needs. Stockholm 4 South is the first data center provider in Europe to achieve the prestigious OCP Ready™ for Hyperscale certification.

Stockholm 4 South prioritizes sustainability. It operates on 100% renewable energy via Vattenfall 24/7 matching and cleverly channels waste heat into Stockholm Exergi's district heating network.  For the security-conscious, the facility offers computer rooms boasting shell protection class 3.

This data center caters to diverse requirements, effortlessly accommodating everything from single racks to purpose-built cages and fully private suites.  Clients can rest assured their data resides in a sustainable, secure, and optimally connected environment.

Some key features include:
- Up to 14 MW of scalable colocation capacity
- Flexible and adaptable design tailored to suit the varied needs of our clients
- Liquid cooling readiness, ready to support the latest in cooling advancements
- AI-ready infrastructure, featuring a slab floor and versatile configurations
- Powered by 100% renewable energy, promoting climate-responsible operations
- Heat reuse connected to the district heating system, ensuring energy is repurposed efficiently
- Enhanced security with Security Class 3 protection

STACK Infrastructure is a leading provider of digital infrastructure to scale the world's most innovative companies. The Company delivers an extensive geographic footprint spanning the United States, Canada, Europe and Asia Pacific and a comprehensive suite of data center and digital infrastructure solutions, including hyperscale campuses and build-to-suit data centers ("HYPERSTACK"), immediately available wholesale colocation and private data suites ("READYSTACK"), and powered shell options ("POWERSTACK").

The STO01 campus is located in Upplands Väsby, a communications center for the northern suburbs of Stockholm. The location enables carrier neutral high speed connectivity. The unique combination of ample connectivity, significant expansion capacity, and state-of-the-art technical solutions ensure the Stockholm site is truly exceptional and sets a new standard for scalable colocation.

STACK STO01A is a highly connected and secure colocation data center at our Stockholm STO01 Campus, powered by 100% renewable energy.

STO01A offers available white space, for both READYSTACK and POWERSTACK solutions.

atNorth offers state-of-the-art facilities for equipment housing. Maintained round the clock by expert staff, all facilities are equipped to the highest industry standards, with multiple redundant fiber connections, 24 hour security, automatic fire suppression, flexible AC and DC power distribution, and uninterruptible power supply (UPS) with backup generators. atNorth customers can house their equipment in shared rooms, cages or private suites in a highly secure managed environment with up to 99.999% availability of power. On-site hands-and-eyes, engineering and maintenance services include preventive, pre-scheduled and rapid-response installation or maintenance work.

Nestled within Stockholm's bustling center, Stockholm 1 City emerges as Conapto's strategically located data center solution. This facility caters perfectly to smaller deployments, ideal for businesses seeking colocation capacity just minutes from the heart of the city.  Additionally, the data center accommodates companies with stringent compliance requirements through its workplace recovery services.  Stockholm 1 City offers scalability, seamlessly accommodating everything from single racks to secure caged solutions.  The data center boasts a single computer room, delivers a 1 MW capacity, and prioritizes sustainability by operating entirely on 100% renewable electricity via Vattenfall's 24/7 matching program.

At GleSYS, we're committed to making our business as sustainable as possible. We've introduced a range of initiatives to minimize the long-term environmental impact. The power in our highly connected carrier neutral facility comes from 100% from renewable sources. It is eco-labeled "Good Environmental Choice" by the Swedish Society for Nature Conservation. Furthermore, our data center's excess heat is being part of keeping its citizens warm year-round through district heating.

Services include but are not limited to Colocation Cages Suites 1/3-Rack Shared Colo-rack 24/7 onsite support 24/7 accessibility Cloud Dedicated Servers VPS Managed Hosting Storage Backup Network Services.

atNorth has announced plans to build a second data center in Stockholm, Sweden, known as SWE02. The facility is scheduled to go live in Q4 2027.

The new site will have a capacity of 30MW and will be located close to the company’s first Swedish data center, SWE01, which opened in March 2022. The project will include a new substation built by Ellevio.

SWE02 will feature heat reuse capabilities through a collaboration with Stockholm Exergi, enabling recycled heat to support local needs and contribute to Sweden’s circular economy.

The existing SWE01 facility is located in Kista, a suburb of Stockholm. It offers 12MW of capacity across 6,400 sqm and currently hosts clients including 6G AI Sweden and AlphaTon Capital Corp.

Our city sites are built to deliver the best of both worlds - instant access and to contribute to more sustainable IT operations. Located in different parts of Stockholm they together constitute one of the market's strongest twin-site alternatives. City Sites are optimized for colocation of data with ultra-low latency. By placing your demanding equipment for applications such as Edge, IoT, and data that require full replication in our City Sites, you can take a big step towards more sustainable IT operations.

The sites are fundamentally designed and built for data center operations and are thus fully optimized for this purpose. Both sites have very advanced shell protection with the aim of creating a safe place for our customers' equipment. In addition to this, the design and construction of the supply systems are based on market-leading technology and redundant systems.

Our data center City 1 is strategically located near Stockholm City and equipped with robust shell protection. The data center is built and designed to create a safe and reliable operating environment, ideal for customers who place high demands on availability and security. With a high level of redundancy in all supply systems, staffing around the clock and a secure energy supply that guarantees the highest level of operational reliability, City 1 is one of Stockholm's most modern data centers. Heat recycling for district heating makes the site a climate-smart alternative, ideal for equipment that needs to be located in Stockholm, or as a complement to our Main Site in Falun.

City 2 has the entire roof filled with solar panels, which makes the data center a climate-smart alternative, ideal for equipment that needs to be located in Stockholm or as a complement to our Main Site in Falun. With a location close to Stockholm city and with robust shell protection, the data center is designed to offer a safe and reliable operating environment that matches high demands on accessibility and security. All supply systems have a high level of redundancy, the center is staffed around the clock and has a secure energy supply that guarantees the highest level of operational reliability.

Located in the technological hub of Kista, north of Stockholm, the Obe Data Center stands as a testament to modern data management and security. With a strategic position between the city and Arlanda airport, it offers high-density co-location services, capable of supporting up to 22 kW per cabinet. The facility boasts redundant cooling systems, UPS, and generators, ensuring uninterrupted service.

Security is paramount, with perimeter access controlled by the property owner and additional surveillance managed by Obe, including camera monitoring and 24/7/365 alarm systems. The data center is also equipped with redundant fiber paths for robust connectivity.

Power and cooling systems are designed for redundancy, with a capacity of 2 MW, three MGE Galaxy 300 kVa UPS units, and three Stamford 700 kVa generators. Cooling is handled by four 80 kW units, and fire prevention is managed with an Inergen system, reflecting Obe's commitment to reliability and safety.

TelecityGroup is Europe's industry-leading provider of premium carrier-neutral data centres. We specialise in the design, build and management of highly-connected, resilient and secure environments for customers to house their telecoms, internet and IT infrastructure.

Our data centres are located in the key European connectivity hubs. A TelecityGroup data centre is a thriving digital ecosystem providing direct access to a wide choice of telecoms and content distribution networks, key internet exchange points and cloud hubs

Bahnhof Data center Sterik features high density cooling and has recently been expanded and mechanically overhauled with new gen set, switch board, etc. Now holds 120+ rack of which 50 can support true high density loads with regards to both power and cooling. True choice of service level, only colocation, colocation with remote hands or managed hosting - it's your choice. 

Please contact or sales department at sales<at>bahnhof.net for more information!

Flexible, scalable power densities & configuration

Because our data centres are based on flexible modular infrastructure, they can easily scale and adapt to your exact requirements and long-term strategic needs.
Flexible, scalable power densities from 1kW of power per rack allow you to have a mix of densities in the same area, only drawing the power you need to each individual rack. All your equipment can be located together, making it easy to manage and eliminating extra interconnects. If you want to add a new server to your rack, you can simply add more power to support it.

Data centers (DCs) are central and vital points in our ecosystem. These are often found locally and are spread out in the different directions: East, West, North, and South.

Thus, it is quite easy to create improved availability, security, redundancy, and really low latency by utilizing those that are closest geographically. We also have the option to offer data centers far away or even in another Nordic country if this is a requirement, for example, for long-term data storage. 

A well-functioning DC always has redundant power and cooling. Stockholm Nord has an impressive capacity of over 1.6 MW from Vattenfall and redundant backup power, 3 x Volvo diesels. The facility is well equipped to be operational regardless of the circumstances. Stockholm Nord stands out with exceptionally climate-smart performance, security, and is certified according to Protection Class 3 (skyddsklass 3) and Alarm Class 3 (larmklass 3).

We want to offer a sustainable future through efficient heat recovery and redundant communication solutions. We offer co-location for both small and large organizations. With over 10,000 available rack spaces, there is room to grow in this data center. We strive for the highest standards in environmental and security standards. We also offer the possibility of a private cage or a separate room.

One of Sweden's most modern data centers deep in the bedrock. The space was originally a military bunker from World War II which was built to withstand nuclear attacks. The center possesses the latest in security, UPS, cooling and electrical power. Of course, all set with redundancy. If the center would be subjected to an extended power outage is also heavy diesel generators on site to keep the stream alive. Besides this, it is also guarded against fire and floods. Obviously, the whole center is video monitored around the clock.

Elementica Data Center is a high-efficiency colocation facility located in Stockholm, Sweden. Developed by Bahnhof, it is designed to be environmentally friendly, utilizing waste heat recovery to contribute to Stockholm’s district heating system. The facility has a 21MW power capacity, ensuring robust infrastructure for hyperscale and enterprise customers.

Elementica is built with CO₂-neutral power sources, offering low electricity costs and high connectivity. The data center integrates advanced security measures, including redundant fiber connections, UPS battery systems, and backup generators for uninterrupted operations. It is also ISO-certified, ensuring compliance with industry-leading standards.

The facility is part of Bahnhof’s expanding portfolio of sustainable data centers, reinforcing Sweden’s position as a leading hub for digital infrastructure. Elementica aims to set a new benchmark in energy efficiency and climate-conscious data center operations.

Servercentralen is the cloud factory for IT professionals. Through our services and experience we enable you or your customer to run an advanced IT infrastructure 24/7.

We are about 30 people divided into several teams in different time zones dedicated to serving our customers around the clock.

Servercentralen provides a full range of managed or un-managed public and private cloud services - virtual servers, hosted mail, remote desktops, backup, domain registration, storage, internet capacity, IPVPN, content delivery network and many more - on a monthly basis and in a scalable solution.

The infrastructure starts off in our own datacenters and spreads via reliable partners in Europe, Asia and US. This way, we keep control of our production and can deliver outstanding support when you need us.

Servercentralen has offices in Stockholm, Sweden and Pune, India and currently serves more than 38.000 users.

Colocation and Hosting with security and energy efficiency in focus. IP-Only offers customers access and support 24/7/365. 2N redundancy is standard in IP-Only's data centers. Optimal uptime is provided through Availo's top ranked IP-net.

At Sätra, IP-Only has implemented an energy efficient system for passive cooling, which radically reduces the need of coolers and fans - saving energy and lowering costs!

T.Loop’s DEC003 is a Data Energy Center® located in the central parts of southern Stockholm. Established in 2024, it has a total capacity of 1.0MW and operates with 30% lower energy costs than traditional data centers. The facility is designed to benefit IT customers, property owners, and energy companies, offering a 100% lower climate CO₂ footprint compared to conventional data centers.

DEC003 is part of T.Loop’s commitment to sustainable digital infrastructure, integrating heat recovery systems and energy-efficient solutions to minimize environmental impact. The facility is strategically positioned in Stockholm, a city known for its high connectivity standards and strong government support for fiber penetration.

As a Data Energy Center®, DEC003 sets a new benchmark for circular energy flows in the data center industry. T.Loop aims to redefine colocation services, ensuring minimal fossil fuel use and optimizing heat recovery for surrounding properties.

T.Loop’s DEC001 is a Data Energy Center® located at The Factory in Häggvik, Stockholm. Established in 2023, it has a total capacity of 0.2MW and operates with 30% lower energy costs than traditional data centers. The facility is designed to benefit IT customers, property owners, and energy companies, offering a 100% lower climate CO₂ footprint compared to conventional data centers.

DEC001 is part of T.Loop’s commitment to sustainable digital infrastructure, integrating heat recovery systems and energy-efficient solutions to minimize environmental impact. The facility is strategically positioned in Stockholm, a city known for its high connectivity standards and strong government support for fiber penetration.

As the world’s first Data Energy Center®, DEC001 sets a new benchmark for circular energy flows in the data center industry. T.Loop aims to redefine colocation services, ensuring minimal fossil fuel use and optimizing heat recovery for surrounding properties.

With its military-grade steel and futuristic design, Lajka combines security and accessibility for businesses that want to grow beyond the common horizons. It's not a coincidence that Lajka is located in Kista Science Park, home of Sweden's most innovative companies.

STACK Infrastructure is a leading provider of digital infrastructure to scale the world's most innovative companies. The Company delivers an extensive geographic footprint spanning the United States, Canada, Europe and Asia Pacific and a comprehensive suite of data center and digital infrastructure solutions, including hyperscale campuses and build-to-suit data centers ("HYPERSTACK"), immediately available wholesale colocation and private data suites ("READYSTACK"), and powered shell options ("POWERSTACK").

The STO01 campus is located in Upplands Väsby, a communications center for the northern suburbs of Stockholm. The location enables carrier neutral high speed connectivity. The unique combination of ample connectivity, significant expansion capacity, and state-of-the-art technical solutions ensure the Stockholm site is truly exceptional and sets a new standard for scalable colocation.

Adjacent to the existing operational data center (STO01A), STO01B offers exceptional connectivity, green power and proximity to the city of Stockholm. With 5,600 sqm of IT white space and up to 18MW of IT power load, this second phase at the Stockholm STO01 campus offers outstanding resiliency, redundancy, and security for your most mission-critical workloads.

STO01B offers white space, for HYPERSTACK & POWERSTACK solutions.

Hosting and co-location services, located centrally in Stockholm city. With redundant power, cooling and internet connections.

We also provide a high security infrastructure, 24/7 support, with the on-site technicians.

Look at our website for further information.

Brookfield Infrastructure Partners is investing up to 95 billion kronor ($9.9 billion) in the Strängnäs AI Data Center located at Gorsingeberget, E20, Strängnäs, Sweden. This project significantly expands the initial plans, increasing the capacity to 750MW from a previously planned 300MW facility. To accommodate this growth, Brookfield intends to acquire an additional 350,000 sqm of land in Strängnäs.

The company anticipates the data center will reach its full capacity within 10-15 years. This development is part of Brookfield's strategy to invest at scale in the infrastructure essential for AI development, including data centers, data transfer, chip storage, and energy generation. The goal is to enhance sovereign compute capabilities for both public services and private enterprises across Europe, supporting Sweden's ambition to become a leading AI hub.
Profile text unavailable for this listing.

The Thule bunker is Stockholm's biggest data center and cuts deep into the Brunkeberg Ridge. Thule is Stockholm's Biggest Fibre Hub. If you rent a server or a rack here, you will get physical access. Thule is one of Sweden's most energy efficient data centers.

The Gullan Cosmic Network Station, located in the heart of Kista's IT cluster, represents the next generation of data centers with a focus on ultra-low latency and strategic peering to ensure the fastest response times in the Nordics. This state-of-the-art facility is integrated with Bahnhof's expansive Nordic fiber network, providing unparalleled connectivity.

As a hub for fiber, light, and collaboration, Gullan offers a unique meeting space, including a spectacular conference room, connected office spaces, and a break area in the space-themed bar. The data center is designed with security and reliability in mind, featuring UPS systems, cooling, shell protection, and redundant power supplies to ensure continuous operation.

Environmentally conscious, Gullan operates on renewable energy and boasts the Triple Green environmental label. In partnership with local power companies, it contributes to heating thousands of homes by recycling excess heat. With its robust security measures, including personal access logging and camera surveillance, Gullan sets a new standard for safe, reliable, and eco-friendly data center operations.
Please visit the website of AB Stokab for further details about Stokab KN7 - Östermalm.
Please visit the website of AB Stokab for further details about Stokab KN6 - Hammarby.
Please visit the website of AB Stokab for further details about Stokab KN3 - Kista.
Please visit the website of AB Stokab for further details about Stokab KN5 - Södermalm.

Our data centres are located in buildings specifically designed to house computer equipment safely and securely. These are situated so that there is no impending threat from floods or other types of natural disaster. They are also located slightly away from densely populated areas. The locations are specially secured against vandalism and intrusion. For customers opting for an extra degree of physical security, our location in an underground facility some distance outside Stockholm can provide a good alternative.

Recent advances in the computing hardware located in data centres create enormous demands for power, cooling and ventilation. Basefarm's data centres are therefore equipped with powerful water conduits for cooling, ventilation systems for removal of dust and moisture, as well as large uninterruptible power supplies (UPS). In addition, they have one or more diesel units that will serve in the event of a major power outage. Development is rapidly moving towards smaller and more power consuming components, which generate much more heat in much less space. This means that a data centre designed only a few years ago cannot provide enough power and cooling to serve more than a small part of the room filled with today’s servers. Every time we put a new server into use, we adjust the conditions in the computer rooms to the new requirements, and when new rooms are built, room for future development is kept on the forefront of planning.

Basefarm currently has servers in several data centres of various sizes and levels of complexity both in Norway and Sweden. This way we can offer our customers the possibility for redundancy in terms of physical location as well. Backups are stored in a separate location to the data centres.
Please visit the website of Orange for further details about Orange STH2.
Profile text unavailable for this listing.

Teracom offers several Datacenter facilities across Sweden. Our facilities are connected to Teracom national backbone by dual redundant fiber access. Each Datacenter provides unique solution for our customer servers based on uninterruptible power supply and cooling system in an environment with highly secure perimeter. We offer everything from high-density racks, cages and private suites tailored to meet our customer's specific demands. Our Datacenter offers:

.	To colocate equipment in the most secure Datacenters environment in Sweden. 

.	Up to 1000 sqm space per site in common area, cage or private suite. 

.	Outlet strips in the form of dual PDUs (measurable if necessary). 

.	Measured dual power supply per feed (A and B power) with backed up by diesel and UPS on both feeds. 


.	Continuously system monitoring of power, temperature, humidity, etc.

.	Highly sensitive sampling detectors react and alert even before visible smoke is formed. Extinguishing System with Novec / Argonit to extinguish the fire by suffocation in less than 60 seconds. 

.	Perimeter protection in safety class 3 with CTTV cameras, alarms and surveillance. 

.	Hands and Eyes by Teracom technicians, available 24/7 365.

EVROC has acquired land in Arlandastad, located north of Stockholm and near Stockholm Arlanda Airport, to develop its first data center. The project, valued at 25 million SEK ($2.2 million, is set to break ground in the first half of 2025, with operations expected to commence by the latter half of 2026. The development will span 3,500 sqm (37,675 sq ft) and is designed to house 16,000 GPUs at full capacity.

The data center aims to be a sustainable and modern cloud-based facility, integrating AI at every layer from operations to developer tools. It will be powered by fossil-free energy and designed to minimize environmental impact through AI-driven optimizations in cooling and resource allocation. This project is part of EVROC's broader plan to develop 10 hyperscale data centers across Europe over the next decade, with an investment of €600 million ($640 million) in this initial site.

TeliaSonera International Carrier offers high-quality data center solutions for customers who value high availability, high security, flexibility and convenience – at affordable prices. You can place your equipment and connect it to TeliaSonera International Carrier’s capacity and IP backbone, and connect to local loop providers and other service providers.
Profile text unavailable for this listing.

Colocation and Hosting with security and energy efficiency in focus. IP-Only offers customers access and support 24/7/365. 2N redundancy is standard in IP-Only's data centers. Optimal uptime is provided through IP-Only's top ranked IP-net. Ideal central location in Hammarby Sjostad!

TeliaSonera International Carrier offers high-quality data center solutions for customers who value high availability, high security, flexibility and convenience – at affordable prices. You can place your equipment and connect it to TeliaSonera International Carrier’s capacity and IP backbone, and connect to local loop providers and other service providers.
Profile text unavailable for this listing.

atNorth is planning a significant expansion with its new SWE04 data center campus in Sollefteå, Sweden. Situated in Hamre Industrial Park, Långsele, the facility will occupy a 50-hectare plot and is slated for operation in the first half of 2028. This development underscores atNorth's commitment to sustainable and scalable digital infrastructure, capable of supporting high-density workloads with direct liquid cooling and densities up to 1MW per rack. The company also intends to pursue heat reuse partnerships for the campus. The Sollefteå Municipality has welcomed the investment, recognizing its potential to strengthen the local economy and create skilled employment opportunities. This new site adds to atNorth's growing portfolio of data centers across the Nordic region.

Let your business develop and grow in our state-of-the-art facility in Falun, where sustainable design and energy efficiency meet optimal physical and technical security. This is Sweden's safest HPC plant, optimized for high-density applications. Our Main Site already protects critical data from some of the world's largest companies in various industries. This is a testament to the site's unique capacity and EcoDataCenter's ability to meet high demands and handle large data volumes.

The extremely high level of security at the facility includes both shell protection and modern security systems as well as 24/7 surveillance. As the site was built, the location was carefully chosen. A barrier of boulders and hard-to-reach areas surrounds the site, which is located in a low risk area outside Falun.

This large-scale facility gives your company the opportunity to quickly gain access to more space as the needs get bigger. The facility's flexible and modern design allows us to offer many different options, ranging from high-density racks in shared environments to wholesale solutions in our own properties. This means that our customers can look forward to a long-term and secure solution for their data operations.

With its unique location in Falun, about 200 km northwest of Stockholm, the site has access to nearby wind farms and hydropower plants. The electricity consumed at our site in Falun is 100% renewable. 75% comes from hydropower and 25% from wind power. The electricity comes from large regional hydropower plants and nearby wind farms that have extremely low CO? emissions during their lifecycle and offer highly competitive energy costs. In the big cities, the energy mix looks completely different. The renewable energy sources that are considered "green" usually has a much larger climate footprint.

The site comprises data centers of varying sizes, with a total of 80 MW of available electrical power for electricity and cooling, redundantly few from independent and separate power stations near the site.

The site is connected to the city of Falun's CHP plant and in a unique solution, the generated surplus energy is used for heating of surrounding properties and production of renewable fuel in the form of pellets.

EcoDataCenter is set to establish a significant presence in Borlänge, Sweden, with the acquisition of a 20-acre site previously occupied by a paper mill plant. This new venture aims to create a 'mega campus' for data centers, with a projected capacity of at least 240MW, potentially expanding up to 360MW.

The strategic location near Falun, where EcoDataCenter already operates, will allow for an efficient expansion of services while leveraging the existing partner ecosystem. This development is poised to make it the largest data center in Sweden, with completion targeted for 2027.

This ambitious project marks a milestone in EcoDataCenter's growth, contributing to the transformation of the Dalarna region into a hub for high-tech infrastructure and sustainable energy solutions.

Hydro66 has changed the economics of colocation by rejecting expensive legacy designs and adopting techniques pioneered by large internet companies for the colocation market for the first time. Located just 30km from Facebook's European datacenter, Hydro66 is the first large-scale colocation provider in the region.

Customers have been able to reduce their carbon footprint and reduce their costs compared to conventional colocation. 

Traditional data centres waste huge amounts of power on cooling equipment such as compressor-based AC. Our location just 60km from the Arctic Circle (latitude 66´ north) means we can rely on natural free air-cooling for 95% of the year and complemented by ultra-efficient evaporative cooling on the warmest days of the year. Our PUE is always less than 1.07 - power conditioning, UPS, air handling and efficient lighting add a maximum of 7% to the core IT load.

* 100% renewable hydroelectric energy 
* 100% free air cooling resulting in a design PUE of 1.07
* Building designed around efficient airflow and integrated into the surrounding environment.
* Located directly adjacent to abundant generation to avoid power transmission losses.

The local power grid has not suffered an outage since 1979.

<h2>A new design to solve a global problem.</h2>

Many of the unique features in the Hydro66 data centre stem from the disruptive design that takes advantage of the local environmental and operational climate to minimise the impact on the environment as well as significantly reducing costs.

The design has four key differences to the 'normal' data centre found in major European cities. These are:

1.	A "spread-out" design taking advantage of the relatively low cost and high availability of land in our location.
The data centre is not a normal multi-floor 'square box' design, but a unique 'spine and branch' design that enables significant innovative ideas for lowering carbon footprint impacts as well as dramatically lowering the overall PUE of the data centre.

2.	The use wherever possible of locally sourced materials and labour to build and operate the data centre.
The design draws on decades of local building experience. All of the major construction materials have been sourced and fabricated in Sweden or the wider Nordic area using 100% local labour for the major superstructure. Where sourcing within the Nordics was not possible, European based companies selected in preference. 

3.	100% "free air" cooling, 100% of the time.
Hydro66 does not use any indirect transfer mediums or 'split' forced chilling units anywhere within the facility. Even in the height of summer when supplemental water based adiabatic cooling is required this is done 'in-hall' and all cooling is housed entirely within the building. This is important to protect from extreme low temperatures and the significant amount of snow that occurs during winter. There is no compromise in terms of the rack environment provided, with temperature and humidity fully controlled within ASHRAE guidelines.

4.	Resilient power designed-in and used as the base line
The facility has the unique attraction of being directly adjacent to a new 120MW substation that is multi dropped from separate regional grid infrastructures. There is a 80MW hydro-electric power station just 500m away with other grid feeds coming from the local, regional and national grids, including the 16 other hydro-electric power stations further up the Lule river which generates a huge 4300MW.

5. Collaboration with manufacturers
In adopting free air cooling and rejecting compressor based or indirect free air solutions Hydro66 needed a solution that would process and filter large volumes of air efficiently and also be able to control humidity.   Through close collaboration with EcoCooling a customised version of the ECT10800 evaporative cooler was created and has been deployed in the datacentre.

Learn more or contact us at www.hydro66.com

HIVE Digital Technologies' 32 MW Boden facility in Sweden is a flagship example of sustainable and efficient data center operations. Powered by hydroelectric energy, the facility supports Sweden's energy infrastructure by providing demand and frequency response services, enhancing grid stability. It also collaborates with the Research Institute of Sweden on innovative projects to improve data center efficiency and sustainability. Beyond its technical achievements, the Boden facility contributes to the local community by sponsoring the Boden Hockey Club and the HIVE Arena, showcasing HIVE's commitment to social and environmental responsibility.

HIVE Digital Technologies' second Boden facility in Sweden is a unique data center originally developed as part of the EU Horizon 2020 initiative. Known as the Boden Type One data center, it was designed to showcase innovative methods for improving data center efficiency. The facility achieved an impressive Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of 1.0148 through advanced cooling techniques, including synchronized IT and cooler fans, and leveraging the cold ambient temperatures. Its modular design featured four pods tailored for different workloads, including Open Compute Project servers, GPUs for rendering, and crypto mining ASICs.

Acquired by HIVE in 2023, the facility is strategically located to support the company's expansion in Boden. This acquisition aligns with HIVE's commitment to sustainable practices and energy efficiency, further strengthening its position in the digital infrastructure and blockchain industries.

Gompute's Data center is specialized on the hosting of large power density racks. Two levels of AC power (diesel generator+UPS, or raw power) are available.

The data center comprises two data rooms of approx 400 and 500 square meters.

Possibility to private cold aisles or private data rooms.

Available office space for operation rooms.

Gothnet DC Marieholm represents a pinnacle of technological advancement and security in data center services. Offering customers the opportunity to rent space for servers and communication equipment, it provides a high-tech environment that ensures safety, scalability, and security. The data center boasts climate control, uninterrupted power supply (UPS), backup power, extinguishing systems, and round-the-clock surveillance by technically skilled personnel. With access to the data center 24/7 and a connection to Gothenburg's city fiber network, clients can maintain control over their equipment while benefiting from the data center's robust infrastructure.

The facility is committed to sustainability, matching its energy consumption with 100% renewable energy purchases, emphasizing its dedication to a sustainable future. Clients can choose from private rooms with custom specifications, rack cabinets in open-plan layouts, or units in shared cabinets, each option providing tailored solutions to meet diverse needs. The data center's service level agreement promises a 99.8% availability, ensuring reliable operations for all hosted equipment.

Gothnet DC Backa is a modern datacenter designed to provide a secure and scalable environment for critical IT infrastructure. It offers a range of services including unit, rack, or private room rentals, tailored to meet diverse client needs.

The facility boasts advanced climate control systems, uninterrupted power supplies, backup generators, and comprehensive fire suppression mechanisms. These features ensure optimal operating conditions and risk mitigation for hosted equipment.

Security is paramount at Gothnet DC Backa, with round-the-clock monitoring of fire, moisture, and intrusion alarms. A technically proficient service desk is available 24/7 to address any concerns and maintain operational excellence.

Connectivity is a key advantage, with the datacenter providing reliable links to other operators and the city's fiber network. This strategic location ensures that clients' servers and communication equipment remain well-connected and accessible.

High security data center with carrier neutral co-location (HPC-support) located in Sweden, between Stockholm and Gothenburg. Single racks, cages from 10 racks, isolated datacenter-in-datacenter from 50 racks.

Complete redundancy, multiple carriers, 24/7 access and remote hands available.

Managed hosting and colocations services in Gothenburg, Sweden since 1995. Our full service, self-owned, data center built 2006 has all the facilities you can expect like redundant cooling, redundant Internet connectivity, protected power and high physical security. We host customers from single servers up to full racks and/or cages on demand. Our managed services include daily maintenence, patching and updating systems, designing networks and serversystems, backups, security services (firewalls etc.)

JBT Datacenter has:

- High security, In order to access the Data Center you have to pass 3 different security cells. 
- Diesel Power
- Redundant UPS 
- Redundant Cooling
- Redundant Fiber communication into the datacenter
- Redundant Upstream providers
- Connected to PINs 10 GB Backbone witch cover most of the West Cost of Sweden.
- Redundant 10 GB Switching arcitecture
- Redundant 24/7 Support

Optinet's datacenter, located in Göteborg, Sweden, stands out as a self-managed facility, ensuring complete control over the entire data hall operation. Since 1997, Optinet has been at the forefront of datacenter management, moving to their own property in Partille in 2010. This move underscores their long-standing experience and commitment to providing secure IT operations.

The datacenter prides itself on high security standards, meeting GDPR requirements, making it a trusted choice for municipalities and public organizations. With all data stored within Sweden, clients benefit from the protection of Swedish law, alongside compliance with stringent IT and information security standards set by financial institutions.

Emphasizing green IT, Optinet's datacenter operates with energy efficiency in mind. Servers are Energy Star certified, and all electricity is sourced from 100% renewable energy. The facility also utilizes virtualization to reduce power consumption, reflecting their dedication to environmentally conscious practices.

Bahnhof is developing Bunkerberget, an underground data center in Gamlestaden, Gothenburg. The facility is housed within a World War II-era bunker, originally blasted as a shelter for Gothenburg residents. During the Cold War, it was further reinforced as an industrial bunker to ensure production for SKF (Svenska Kullagerfabriken) in case of war.

The mountain room spans 6,000 square meters with a nine-meter ceiling height, while the entire site covers 28,000 square meters. Bahnhof acquired the property from Bunkeberget AB, though financial details were not disclosed. Construction is expected to begin after the summer of 2025, transforming the site into a secure digital hub.

Bunkerberget will be Bahnhof’s seventh data center and second bunker facility, joining its Pionen data center, which is located in a nuclear-proof bunker in Stockholm. Bahnhof CEO Jon Karlung envisions Gothenburg as Europe’s digital port, combining robust communication infrastructure with a human-centered approach.

Ember's Skövde Data Center is part of Ember's network of data centers in northern Europe. It offers a range of services including Cloud compute, Bare-metal servers, Co-location, and AI GPU compute. The facility is designed with sustainability in mind, running solely on 100% renewable energy and featuring energy-efficient cooling systems. It also boasts N+1 redundancy for high reliability and protection.
Profile text unavailable for this listing.

Hostek’s data center services prioritize security, sustainability, and global reach. Designed for enterprise-grade performance, their facilities are powered entirely by 100% renewable energy, with backup systems running on eco-friendly fuels. Energy efficiency is embedded in the architecture, with redundant N+1 systems across power, cooling, and connectivity ensuring uninterrupted operations.

The company maintains a global infrastructure, enabling geographically distributed redundancy and hybrid integrations with clients’ existing systems—whether on-premises or in third-party environments. Security measures include Class 3 perimeter protection, surveillance with logging, and automated fire suppression systems compliant with EI120 standards.

These data centers don’t just meet compliance—they invite trust, offering guided tours and transparent engagement to reinforce confidence. For businesses seeking resilient and environmentally conscious infrastructure, Hostek blends technical rigor with a personal touch.

Located in the vibrant city of Malmö, the DC Malmö Scheelegatan datacenter, operated by 31173 Services AB, stands as a testament to modern technology and efficiency. Offering a suite of services including colocation, dark fiber, and customized solutions, this facility is designed to meet the high demands for security, availability, and capacity. With its strategic position in southern Sweden, it serves as a crucial hub for data and connectivity solutions.

Ideal Co-Location in Northern Europe. Located in an old telecommunications central in Malmö, our newest data center boasts an ideal co-location infrastructure; this is where Europe's and Scandinavia's fibres meet. That's why it's called ideal Co-Location in Northern Europe.

Colocation at Bredband2 involves intrusion, fire protection, emergency power, proximity and minimal environmental impact. Unlike many other players in the market, we run everything from our own Data Centers, instead of hiring us in others. 

Via our Datacenter in Stockholm, Malm´ and Ume´ we take care of the environment around your server by delivering maximum uptime with redundant power and cooling, and full perimeter security. We can supply Colocation space for everything from a server to a completely private room that is exactly tailored to your needs. You are responsible for building up your environment, as you may desire, while we take care of all other operational works flawlessly. The environment is connected to the Internet via Bredband2's core network and / or directly to your business. You can access your equipment around the clock, every day.

The sites' flexible design can be easily adapted for low- and high-density alternatives on a small or large scale. Our offerings include custom solutions for larger customers in private rooms (Customized Private Rooms), private suites in shared rooms (Customized Private Suites), and Customized Cages.

In addition to its physical location, which creates an optimal climate for data center operations, the site offers other advantages, such as the area's excellent access to renewable electricity. The large supply enables the area to offer electricity from 100% hydropower at prices that are among the lowest in the world.

Our Arctic Site 1 consists of two data centers, both built in the same area and equipped with the same advanced shell protection. The location is a low-risk area, carefully chosen for its accessibility, excellent power supply, and secure communication. The northern location contributes to efficient cooling and access to 100% renewable electricity.
The design is optimized for traditional colocation, HPC, and storage. The design is focused on high availability in a safe and efficient environment.

Built for traditional colocation, HPC, and storage.

Arctic site 2 is an EMP-protected facility located in a safe place with a perfect distance to Arctic site 1, for the sites to constitute a twin site solution. Arctic site 2 is built as a secret military facility on a location that is particularly selected to provide maximum security. The site is ideal for colocation and wholesale services. In addition, the site also offers workplaces that can be used in the event of serious incidents. The northern climate in combination with the free cooling and the easy access to 100% renewable electricity makes the site a perfect choice for efficient data operations.

Designed for traditional Colocation and storage.

Fortlax hosts its services in one of the most secure and sophisticated data centers in Scandinavia. The facility is fully redundant in terms of power, cooling and internet access and a location close to the polar circle reduces the energy required for cooling. In addition to meeting the highest standards in physical and perimeter protection it is also shielded against Compromising Emanations (CE, Swedish: RöS) as well as Electro Magnetic Pulse (EMP). The facility is designed to withstand physical attacks (gas, firing etc) as well as enduring total isolation from the outside world for an extended period of time.

We provide hosting capabilities for SMBs and resellers. We are a professional datacenter with raised floor, rittal racks and APC power and network distribution system. Dual path network, three way power to each rack, central UPS and backup generator to secure power. Dual cooling, early fire detection system, constantly monitored climate. Video surveillance of facility, both inside and outside. Patroling guard outside business hours, on site staff during business hours.

DC Lund Mobilvägen is the latest addition to 31173 Services' datacenter portfolio. Located in the vibrant city of Lund, this facility boasts a capacity of over 100 racks and a power load exceeding 500 kW. The datacenter is designed with extreme security measures and full redundancy on all critical components, ensuring uninterrupted service.

The UPS systems are configured in an N+1 setup with A+B power feeds to each rack. The incoming high voltage is supplied from two separate sources to a dedicated substation and transformer station. Additionally, a 1.1 MW diesel generator guarantees a reliable power supply for over a week in case of outages.

QuickNet's datacenter, located in the heart of Västerås, offers a state-of-the-art facility adhering to the highest Tier-3 standards. The datacenter boasts a combination of UPS systems with extended battery life and a modern backup power generator, ensuring uninterrupted service.

The experienced staff at QuickNet is considered the cornerstone of the datacenter's operational reliability. Their expertise is a critical factor in managing the sophisticated technical equipment effectively.

With a focus on cost-efficiency and smart design, QuickNet provides space in their datacenter at very reasonable prices. Whether hosting a single server or hundreds, each client benefits from the same high level of reliability and security in this top-modern environment.

ARN data centers are part of the eu-north-1 AWS region: Europe (Stockholm).

Construction of this Swedish AWS data center started in 2017. It is located in Kvastbruket, an industrial area of Västerås. The plot is 30 hectars and the plan is to construct 4 data halls of 200 x 60 meters, with a total of 72 diesel generators.

The Ansluten datacenter in Karlshamn represents a pinnacle of modern technology and security. Established in 2014, this facility spans a total area of 100 square meters, with 50 square meters dedicated to the data hall. It is equipped with a Pyramid DSP 30K UPS system and traditional cooling methods to ensure optimal performance of the servers.

Security is paramount at the Karlshamn datacenter. Access is controlled by a centralized pass system, with all entries and exits logged. Surveillance cameras monitor every space, providing an additional layer of security. The premises are also protected by alarm systems and round-the-clock guard patrols, ensuring the safety of the data stored within.

Technical innovation and robust security measures make the Ansluten datacenter in Karlshamn a reliable choice for hosting services. With its state-of-the-art facilities, it stands as a testament to Ansluten Hosting's commitment to excellence in data management and protection.

ScandiDC I, the flagship data center of Scandinavian Data Centers (SDC), is located in Eskilstuna, Sweden, approximately 100 kilometers from Stockholm. This facility is housed in a former military aircraft engine bunker, originally built during World War II, offering unparalleled physical security. Designed to support high-density workloads, ScandiDC I is equipped with advanced infrastructure tailored for AI and HPC (High-Performance Computing) applications, including customized power delivery, cooling systems, and fiber networks.

The data center is a model of sustainability, integrating circular practices by reusing waste heat in district heating networks and other applications, benefiting local communities. With a strong focus on compliance, ScandiDC I meets rigorous international standards such as ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 27001, and PCI-DSS. Its strategic location provides excellent connectivity to global fiber networks and proximity to hyperscalers, making it an ideal hub for secure, efficient, and environmentally responsible data operations.

The Marviken Data Center by EdgeMode is a high-density AI and HPC facility located in Sweden, designed for ultra-efficient scalability. The site provides 95MW of renewable energy, with future expansion beyond 500MW, ensuring sustainable power for AI-driven workloads.

Marviken features next-generation immersion cooling, eliminating traditional air-based cooling systems and reducing operational costs. It also integrates seawater cooling infrastructure, lowering capital expenditure while optimizing heat dissipation. Excess heat is repurposed through district heating, supporting local community sustainability efforts.

The facility is strategically built for low-latency AI processing, featuring direct fiber connectivity for seamless AI inference and machine learning workflows. A fully executed Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) secures long-term energy availability, making Marviken an ideal hub for enterprises requiring sovereign AI hosting and high-performance computing.

EdgeConneX is developing a significant data center campus in Skellefteå, Sweden, with plans for up to 1GW of capacity. This marks EdgeConneX's first development in Sweden, strategically located in a region known for its access to renewable energy, a stable power grid, and a skilled technical workforce. The site, acquired from Lyten, is part of a larger industrial hub that will co-locate battery manufacturing with AI data centers and other industrial operations. EdgeConneX emphasizes sustainability in its planning and development, aligning with the evolving needs of AI and cloud customers. This expansion into Sweden underscores the country's attractiveness for digital infrastructure investment and its potential to advance Sweden's digital competitiveness and AI development in Europe.

At GleSYS, we're committed to making our business as sustainable as possible. We've introduced a range of initiatives to minimize the long-term environmental impact. The power in our highly connected carrier neutral facility comes from 100% from renewable sources. It is eco-labeled "Good Environmental Choice" by the Swedish Society for Nature Conservation. Furthermore, our data center's excess heat is being part of keeping its citizens warm year-round through district heating.

Services include but are not limited to Colocation Cages Suites 1/3-Rack Shared Colo-rack 24/7 onsite support 24/7 accessibility Cloud Dedicated Servers VPS Managed Hosting Storage Backup Network Services.

Cygrids provides a high availability managed datacenter facility including critical infrastructure systems, like power and cooling. The target availability level is 100 percent uptime, excluding planned maintenance windows. 
Access to the Datacenter is restricted and monitored.
Critical infrastructure includes functioning of all power and HVAC (Heating, Ventilation, and Air conditioning) infrastructure including UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply), PDUs (Power Distribution Unit) and cabling, which form part of the co-location facility. 
Infrastructure downtime exists when a particular electronic is shut down due to power or heat problems and Cygrids records such failure in the Cygrids monitoring system.

Ansluten Hosting's Växjö datacenter represents a pinnacle of modern data storage and security. Constructed in 2011-2012, the facility spans 150 square meters within a larger 250 square meter area, dedicated to providing top-tier colocation services.

Security is paramount, with a centralized access system recording all entries and exits, complemented by continuous CCTV surveillance. Intrusion alarms are installed in all areas, ensuring immediate response and regular patrols outside business hours. Access to colocation racks is restricted to respective owners, while other equipment like routers and dedicated servers are housed in a special area accessible only to authorized personnel.

The technical infrastructure is robust, featuring an APC Symmetra PX 250 UPS system and an AQS/Climaveneta free cooling system with a 243 kW cooling capacity. In the event of a fire, the data halls are equipped with early detection smoke detectors and an automatic gas-based extinguishing system using Novec™ 1230 agent, safeguarding the vital electronic equipment within.

Tällberg is a very nostalgic village in the heart of Sweden. The village has a population of a few hundred but because of its idyllic environment it's a very popular tourist destination from what caused 8 hotels appare in the village.

Our data center in Tällberg is built as a concrete vault with complete redundancy of cooling, electricity and fiber optical connectivity.

The datacenter allows you to rent either a single unit co-location or a full cabinet of 48U for very competitive prices.

Located in Luleå, Sweden, the Meta Luleå Data Center leverages the region's cool climate and abundance of renewable energy sources. This substantial facility delivers the power capacity required to support Meta's operations while demonstrating a commitment to sustainable growth.   The Luleå Data Center strategically positions Meta within Scandinavia and the broader Northern European market.

Internetport offers a robust colocation server solution that provides clients with security and flexibility. With 24/7 personal access to data centers, all activities are meticulously logged and monitored to ensure the highest security standards. In case of emergencies, expert help is readily available for both consultation and remote assistance.

The colocation service is supported by a powerful network, boasting up to 10+ Tbps of bandwidth capacity and core network uptime of 99.999%. This ensures blazing speed and reliability for your critical operations. Additionally, Internetport's network is operator neutral, offering connectivity options up to 10 Gbit/s and several Internet exchange points (IX) for optimal performance.

For those seeking seamless integration with cloud services, Internetport provides a hybrid architecture. This allows direct connection to the public cloud, creation of a private network, and connection to other Internetport services. The datacenter also features effortless automation through a user-friendly customer portal and a full-featured API, giving clients complete control over their infrastructure.

15 kW
Traditional cooling solutions can handle only 3-4 kW per rack. A rack full of 40 pieces of 1U servers pull from 5-15 kW of electricity, depending on model and load. Our rack can handle these loads fully.

Higher uptime
Power supply consists of two galvanically separated systems. Each system can support the entire data center load. This is true 2N redundancy - fault on one of the systems does not affect the feeding from the other system. Expected downtime is reduced to 1 / 2000 of a single Power supply with UPS and diesel generator. Similarly, all other systems redundant.

Energy-efficient data center
Computer Center is the most energy efficient in Sweden thanks to a unique cooling system and an electricity infrastructure with reduced losses. A land of 3000 m2 cooling system using ground cooling with superior energy efficiency. We have 60% lower power consumption compared to a conventional data center.

Superior Uptime
Power supply to the rack is made from two (A + B) galvanically separated electrical system (2N) the whole way. If one of the systems do not work the other system takes over the full load from the system.
Expected downtime for 2N the power supply is reduced to 1 / 2000 of a single Power supply (1N) with UPS and diesel generator.
All racks will be supplied with 15 kW A + B UPS power.
Simple logical structure to eliminate handling errors.
Electric rooms for A and B systems are physically separated.
Separated batterie rooms.
Maximum protection, consisting of fixed extinguishing systems with particle sensors and light guard. 

Powerline
11 kV loop.
Uniform and easily operated medium voltage switchgear.
Transformers with reduced power losses.
Oversized switchgear provides low power dissipation.
Low-voltage switchgear are supplied from transformers.

Backup
Backup sets with diesel engines from Volvo Penta of 810 kW hk/600.
The engines are preheated. Battery charger ensures that starting batteries are fully charged.
Starts within 5 seconds after power outage and provides full power after 20 seconds. Meanwhile UPS feed the servers.
Capacity 6 x 550 kW at full extension.

UPS
All electrical power to the rack PDUs goes through the facility UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply). UPS delivers pure AC and have a perfect sine wave.
UPS also protects against a variety of various problems in the grid, such as voltage spikes during a lightning storm.

The batteries have a capacity of 13-14 minutes operation and are placed in their own compartment.
Critical pumps and fans in the coolingsystem is feed trough UPS.
Up to 94.5% efficiency gives • significant energy savings.
Capacity 6 x 360 kW UPS power at full extension.

Power distrubution
Completely galvanically separated power distrubution for A-and B-power.
Ceramic Fuses (16A and 32A) in stead of miniature circuit breakers:
- Fast - 10 times as fast, which protects servers better.
- Precise - no need for oversizing.
- Reliable - no moving parts and no need for maintenance.

PDU
Each rack has two PDU: s, one for A-and B-force, each one capable of addressing the whole load:
- 32A (1 phase and 3 phase) PDU am.
- 16A (1 phase and 3 phase) PDU am.
Different types of PDU: s: 
- Basic - simple.
- Metered - Remote monitoring in real time by connected loads.
- Switched - Measurement combined with controlled on / off switching.


HACS
The hot air is contained in a HACS (Hot Aisle Containment System), with roofs and doors.
The temperature in the cold aile is 22-23 ° C and in the hot aile, it can be 10-20 ° C higher. Server Manufacturer recommends that the supply air should contain 18-27o C.
More free cooling can be used when higher temperatures are used in chillers, which provide superior energy efficiency and high cooling capacity.
Sensors in each rack control air flow and cooling for stable temperatures and air flows to the server regardless of processor load.

Ground Cooling
3000 m2 large ground cooling system that can provide more than 1 MW in a separate circuit.
Ground cooling system is completely independent and flat heat exchangers transfer the cold to InRow Coolers.
1 kW input power for the pumps give • 100 kW cooling, or 30 times as efficient as compressor operation.

Connectivity
Fully redundant fiber paths into the data center.
Two different cross-links in two different fire cells for fiber and copper connections.
Possibility to connect rack with redundant Cat5 and fiber (MM and SM) cables.
Redundant 10 Gbit / s connection to Phonera IP network with MPLS technology directly to Stockholm and Malmo. 

Phonera has a large global IP network with Cisco equipment and peers with a large number of ISPs in many of the largest Global peering points.

Phonera is ranked at the very top of Swedish Internet service providers by the International
CAID organization that evaluates 26,000 different ISPs in the world. Phonera is ranked top 50 in the world.

Nordlo's data center in Lycksele, Sweden, was certified with the Fossil Free Data label in 2021. This is the first such certification in northern Sweden and demonstrates Nordlo's efforts for a sustainable and environmentally friendly data center. 

Nordlo offers data management, virtualization, dedicated servers and colocation services. They also offer secure cloud services with built-in compliance.

Binero enables sustainable digital development for companies and organizations with qualified needs in a wide range of cloud and IT infrastructure solutions. Our data center uses renewable energy and generates reusable heat for nearby residents. Binero STO1 was the first datacenter in the world to receive the Fossil Free Data Center certification.

Whether you want to use your own hardware, colocation, or run your applications in our public cloud, we offer secure and straightforward Swedish cloud services from our data center outside Stockholm.

All parts of the infrastructure for energy and cooling are redundant according to level A+B. This means that the STO1 has two separate power supply systems and two separate cooling supply systems, each of which is redundant. The systems are designed to individually handle the entire IT load, if necessary. The cooling systems are also redundantly built. Cooling circuit A takes its primary coolant from heat pumps connected to the district heating network and its secondary coolant from free cooling units located on the property. Cooling circuit B is identically structured but with completely separate equipment. 

In terms of capacity, each rack has two supplies of 32 amps. This means that the maximum power that can be loaded in a rack is 22kw with full A+B redundancy or 44kw with standard N+1 redundancy (diesel and UPS).

Cabling between racks is provided entirely according to the customer's request or according to our standard. All the network (both ﬁber and copper) are measured to the strictest standards and logged to ensure that cabling is never a source of error.
